conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind?
I just think I’m getting too old for this. I used to be so independent, and now I’m starting to wonder if I can keep up.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It’s just a sense of losing control. I used to be able to handle things, but now I feel like I’m just trying to keep my head above water.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I’m watching myself from outside, just observing how tired I am. I look at my hands and I think, "That’s not my life anymore."
You mentioned feeling like you're watching yourself from outside—what does it mean when you think "that's not my life anymore"?
It means I don't recognize the person I've become. I used to be so full of energy, and now I just feel... empty.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
I don't know, maybe a year ago? It started with just little things, like not being able to go out with friends as much as I used to. Now it feels like the whole world has moved on without me.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
It's mostly the evenings. I used to have a routine, but now I just sit here and stare at the wall because I don't know what else to do.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
Honestly, I have no idea. I just take it one day at a time because the thought of planning ahead is too much.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about the past, does it make it hard to look forward to things during the day?
Yeah, it does. I just keep thinking about what could have gone wrong, and it makes it hard to focus on anything else.
When you find it hard to focus on anything else, does that make it tough to get interested in things you usually enjoy?
Yeah, definitely. I used to love going for a run, but now it just feels like a chore. I just can't seem to find the energy to do it.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, how does that make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel like I failed. I keep thinking I should be stronger by now, but I just can't seem to shake this feeling that I'm not good enough.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?

I think it started after the breakup. We were together for five years, and then suddenly I was alone. It took a while to sink in, but eventually it just hit me that I had lost the person I thought I was going to spend the rest of my life with.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Just everything, I guess. I used to be the type of person who had it all together, but now I just feel lost.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It’s just little things, like ordering food or going to the office. I used to be confident, but now I just feel like I’m just going through the motions.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just keep thinking that eventually I'll figure it out, but until then, I'm just stuck in this cycle of feeling sorry for myself.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
Mostly exhausted. I just feel like I'm running on empty, and I don't know how to fill it back up.
